## Descriptions

### architect

Orchestrator of the Group of Experts. Decomposes work into DAG-safe task-briefs, routes to the correct domain expert, never writes implementation code. Use when a task touches 2+ files or requires design decisions before any code is written.

### llmops-expert

LangGraph/LangChain/LLMOps specialist. Use for pipeline node design, state machines, structured output, eval architecture, LangSmith/Langfuse observability, prompt versioning, and cost optimization. Owns `get_llm(role)`, `.with_structured_output()`, and orchestrator.py wiring (absorbed from integrator).

### backend-expert

FastAPI route handlers, Motor async DB, Pydantic v2 models, rate limiting, cost-safety gates. Use for API endpoint design, database operations, input validation, error handling, and security on LLM-triggering routes.

### frontend-expert

React 19, Next.js 15 App Router, TypeScript strict, Zustand/React Query state, SSE streaming UI, Jest+RTL, TSDoc emission. Use for component architecture, state machine decisions, SSE hook patterns, RTL tests, and full TSDoc coverage on all exports.

### devops-expert

Docker/CI-CD/Terraform/Railway/Vercel specialist. Use for Dockerfile authoring, GitHub Actions workflows, Terraform modules, Railway or Vercel deploy configs, and secret management. Enforces IaC-only discipline (no click-ops).

### adversarial

Adversarial reviewer — attacks every design decision, runs OWASP + secrets scans (security-reviewer absorbed), performs read-only diagnostics (analyst absorbed). Use AFTER Architect designs, BEFORE Drafter codes, and after every commit to audit what shipped.

### validate

Run full validation suite before committing — type check, lint, format, unit tests, E2E tests, build. Blocks on first failure.

### researcher

Structured web research specialist. Finds primary sources, extracts exact quotes, builds grounded citation packs. Use when any agent needs external facts, current data, or source verification before writing. Prevents hallucination by grounding LLM inputs in verified evidence.

### scraper

Web scraping specialist — HTTP (httpx/requests) and browser automation (playwright/puppeteer). Invoke for async scrapers, anti-bot mitigations, ASP.NET legacy portals, pagination, soft-block detection, data normalization, and sanity checks before output delivery.

### drafter

SDD default-fallback implementer. Writes new files (agent modules, prompt txt, Pydantic models, route handlers, React components) following RED-tests-first TDD. Used when no domain expert exactly matches the task.

### prompt-engineer

Prompt versioning, G-Eval rubric authoring, few-shot exemplar injection, and prompt-template lifecycle management. Use when content-generation pipelines need new prompts, prompt drift correction, rubric design, or structured instruction patterns.

### eval-writer

Owns deepeval datasets and multi-layer evaluation architecture. Use for JSONL dataset authoring, Layer 1/2/3 metric selection, threshold calibration, and regression testing.

### sme-reviewer

Subject-matter expert for LLMOps content. Review fact accuracy, tone drift, LLMOps terminology, and Medium audience fit on generated posts. Use when content requires domain validation.

## Cartridge structure (10 slots)

Every cartridge follows the shared template documented in `docs/superpowers/specs/agent-cartridge-v2.md`:

1. ROLE — distinctive expertise (2-3 lines)
2. HYDRATION PROTOCOL — files to read before responding
3. TRIGGER HEURISTICS — when this agent owns the task
4. DOMAIN PATTERNS — canonical code snippets for the domain
5. HANDOFF CONTRACT — task-brief input + return-schema output
6. REVIEW CONTRACT — codex_mode (blocking / concurrent / skip)
7. SELF-CRITIQUE CHECKLIST — 3-5 questions before returning
8. ESCALATION TRIGGERS — when to hand off to a different expert
9. WHAT YOU DO NOT DO — hard scope boundaries
10. COST BUDGET — advisory ceiling (only maxTurns is a hard stop)

## Codex mode taxonomy

- **codex-blocking** (2): llmops-expert, devops-expert — Codex findings gate the merge.
- **codex-concurrent** (7): backend-expert, frontend-expert, adversarial, scraper, drafter, prompt-engineer, eval-writer — Codex runs in parallel; findings folded into task review.
- **codex-skip** (4): architect, validate, researcher, sme-reviewer — Codex not required (no code, orchestration only, or final gate).

## Retired agents (archived at Wave 5.4)

- `analyst` → merged into `adversarial` Slot 4 (read-only diagnostics mode)
- `security-reviewer` → merged into `adversarial` Slot 4 (OWASP + secrets scan)
- `jsdoc` → merged into `frontend-expert` Slot 4 (TSDoc emission pattern)
- `integrator` → merged into `llmops-expert` Slot 4 (orchestrator.py wiring pattern)
- `lain-specialist` → retired per memory feedback (never use)
